title: Syntherisma tenuispica
taxonomy: Syntherisma tenuispica belongs to the family Crambidae, a group of moths known as grass moths or snout moths. The genus Syntherisma is a part of this family, and it includes various species of moths that are often found in grasslands and open areas. These moths are characterized by their elongated bodies and long proboscis, which they use to feed on plant nectar. Syntherisma tenuispica specifically is a species of moth that is native to certain parts of the world, where it inhabits areas with tall grasses and wildflowers. The classification of Syntherisma tenuispica reflects its unique characteristics and habits within the broader family of Crambidae.
